_schema.description: New Zealand trades-vendor coverage. Smaller market than US/UK/AU/CA but with strong NZ-built FSM ecosystem (Fergus, NextMinute, Tradify) and unique licensing landscape (EWRB, PGDB, LBP).

vendors.name: Fergus
vendors.tagline: NZ-built FSM (Auckland) — founded by an NZ plumber, 20K+ trades businesses NZ+AU
vendors.market_position: Fergus is the NZ trades-software success story — founded by a working NZ plumber, now 20K+ trades businesses across NZ + AU. Strongest job-costing and profitability features in the NZ/AU market. Native ACC, GST, PAYE handling, deep Xero/MYOB integration.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Founder is a working NZ plumber — product understanding of NZ trades workflow is genuine. ACC (Accident Compensation Corporation) handling native. NZ support timezone.
pros: Founder is working NZ plumber — domain expertise real
Strongest job-costing depth in NZ/AU market
cons: Best fit for businesses serious about job-profitability analysis (steeper learning curve)
Less specialized than Tradify for pure quoting/invoicing workflow

vendors.name: NextMinute
vendors.tagline: NZ-built job management — 5,000 NZ/AU tradies, 4.9★ Google/Facebook, strong for residential builders
vendors.market_position: NextMinute is NZ-built job management software with 5,000+ NZ/AU tradies. Particularly strong fit for residential builders and carpenters needing project costing. 4.9★ Google + Facebook ratings — among the highest-rated NZ-origin FSM platforms by users.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Strongest residential-builder and carpentry focus among NZ FSM platforms. NZ support + ACC/GST/PAYE handling.
cons: Best fit for project-based trades vs pure service trades

vendors.name: Tradify (NZ)
vendors.tagline: NZ-built FSM with global presence (NZ/AU/UK/US) — easiest onboarding of the NZ-origin platforms
vendors.market_position: Tradify is NZ-built and now operates globally (NZ/AU/UK/US). For NZ contractors, it represents the NZ-origin FSM that has scaled internationally. Easiest onboarding of the NZ-origin platforms. PartnerStack affiliate ($160/signup).
vendors.nz_specific_note: NZ home-market product with global expansion proves the platform. NZ support + ACC/GST/PAYE compliance.
pros: Easiest onboarding among NZ FSM platforms
cons: Less specialized than Fergus for job-costing depth
Pricing higher than ServiceM8 at entry tier

vendors.name: Builderscrack
vendors.tagline: NZ's leading trades marketplace — pay-per-lead model, NZ-only consumer audience
vendors.market_position: Builderscrack is the leading NZ-only trades marketplace. Smaller scale than UK Checkatrade or AU Hipages reflects NZ's smaller market size, but the platform dominates NZ consumer search for trades. Pay-per-lead model.
vendors.nz_specific_note: NZ-only — no cross-market presence. Smaller pool of registered tradies (~50K) than UK/AU equivalents.
pros: Leading NZ trades marketplace by consumer audience
Pay-per-lead model means no annual commitment

vendors.name: NZI Insurance
vendors.tagline: 160-year IAG-owned NZ insurance brand — tradie packages standard
vendors.market_position: NZI is one of NZ's longest-established insurance brands — 160-year history, IAG-owned. Tradie packages cover Public Liability, Tools, Vehicle. Strong claims infrastructure backed by IAG's NZ market dominance.
vendors.nz_specific_note: IAG ownership provides institutional stability. Public Liability typically NZ$1-5M coverage standard.
vendors.typical_pricing: NZ$30-$100/month typical for tradies PL + tools

vendors.name: Vero Insurance
vendors.tagline: Major NZ underwriter — all-in-one tradies package, broker-distributed
vendors.market_position: Vero is one of NZ's major insurance underwriters — known for all-in-one tradies packages distributed through broker channels (Crombie Lockwood, Gallagher NZ). Strong fit for NZ tradies wanting bundled coverage in a single policy.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Broker-distributed primarily — not direct quoting. Crombie Lockwood and Gallagher NZ are common Vero brokers for tradies.
pros: Major NZ underwriter with mature claims

vendors.name: AMI Business
vendors.tagline: NZ IAG retail brand — Tradies Bundle accessible direct online
vendors.market_position: AMI is IAG's NZ retail brand — same parent as NZI but more consumer-facing. Tradies Bundle is direct-online quoting accessible for solo and small NZ trades. Lower pricing than NZI broker-distributed for simple cases.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Direct-online accessibility makes AMI a quick-quote option vs broker-distributed alternatives.
cons: Less coverage depth than broker-distributed Vero

vendors.name: iPayroll
vendors.tagline: NZ-built payroll — full PAYE/IRD compliant, cloud-native
vendors.market_position: iPayroll is NZ-built cloud payroll — full PAYE compliance with IRD (Inland Revenue Department), KiwiSaver handling, ACC levy calculation native. Strong fit for NZ SMB trades businesses wanting NZ-specific compliance without Xero subscription dependency.
vendors.nz_specific_note: IRD-compliant PAYE submissions native. KiwiSaver employer contributions handled. ACC levy auto-calculated.
pros: Cloud-native vs legacy NZ payroll options
cons: Pricing higher than free competitors (Thankyou Payroll)

vendors.name: Smartly
vendors.tagline: NZ payroll specialist — SMB focus, IRD-compliant
vendors.market_position: Smartly is NZ payroll specialist with SMB focus. Full IRD compliance, KiwiSaver, leave management. Competes with iPayroll in the NZ SMB payroll segment.
vendors.nz_specific_note: NZ SMB focus with full compliance. Comparable to iPayroll in core feature set.
cons: Smaller scale than iPayroll or Xero NZ

vendors.name: EWRB (Electrical Workers Registration Board)
vendors.tagline: NZ electrical worker registration — ~38K registered, legally required
vendors.market_position: EWRB is NZ's mandatory electrical worker registration body. ~38,000 registered electrical workers in NZ. Registration is legally required for any electrical work — unlicensed work is criminal offense. EWRB issues different practitioner categories (Electrician, Electrical Service Technician, Electrical Inspector, etc.).
vendors.nz_specific_note: Registration is legally mandatory. Annual fees apply. Practising license required to actually work — different from registration.
pros: Legally mandatory — clear NZ regulatory pathway
cons: Annual fees + practising license costs stack
Practising license must be renewed regularly
No exceptions — every NZ electrical worker must be registered
vendors.typical_pricing: Annual registration + practising license fees vary

vendors.name: PGDB (Plumbers, Gasfitters and Drainlayers Board)
vendors.tagline: NZ plumbing/gas/drainage licensing — legally required, unlicensed work is illegal
vendors.market_position: PGDB is NZ's mandatory plumbing, gasfitting, and drainage licensing body. Legally required for any plumbing/gas/drainage work in NZ — unlicensed work is criminal offense. Issues licenses across three disciplines with separate certifications for each.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Equivalent of UK Gas Safe + plumbing licensing combined. Mandatory licensing across plumbing + gas + drainage disciplines.
cons: Annual fees per discipline add up
Strict enforcement — non-compliance has criminal consequences
